title: Fishing crew rescued near Gloucester after boat runs aground amid harsh seas
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Two crew members rescued from a fishing boat aground on a rocky Gloucester shore by local emergency services.
First responders, including the US Coast Guard, assisted in the rescue near 252 Atlantic Road, Gloucester.
The fishing boat Legacy sustained damage from 2 to 4-foot waves but the crew was uninjured.

title: The Rafael Devers trade is already haunting the Red Sox in 2026
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Rafael Devers was traded to the Giants in 2025, but Boston has struggled to gain value from the trade returns.
The Red Sox, currently 2-7 in 2026, face challenges with poor offensive output and defensive errors.
Boston used financial flexibility to sign Ranger Suarez, but his early-season performance has been disappointing.

title: Why it might be time for a slumping Bruins team to call up James Hagens
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins have scored only three goals in their last three games, threatening their playoff momentum.
James Hagens, a 19-year-old forward from Providence Bruins, is being considered for an NHL call-up to boost the team's offense.
The Bruins' slumping power play is converting only 15.6% of opportunities, ranking 28th in the NHL over the last 20 games.

title: Tatum, Celtics pull away late to beat Raptors, move closer to 2-seed: 6 takeaways
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Boston Celtics secured a 113-95 victory against the Toronto Raptors, moving closer to securing the 2-seed in the playoffs.
Jayson Tatum and Jaylen Brown led the scoring, with Brown contributing a team-high 26 points on 11-for-20 shooting.
Neemias Queta showcased significant improvement with 18 points, 7 rebounds, and 3 blocks, making a case for Most Improved Player.

title: New Bedford man gets decades-long prison sentence for raping young relative
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Daniel Turcios Torres sentenced to 36-45 years in prison for raping young relative in New Bedford.
Turcios Torres charged with multiple counts, including rape of a child aggravated by age and witness intimidation.
The victim reported the assaults in 2024, revealing abuse began after moving from Ecuador in 2019.

title: She is running Boston in honor of her mother who died of brain cancer
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Madison Oakes, a 23-year-old from Boxford, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon in honor of her late mother.
Oakes' mother died of Stage 4 brain cancer, receiving extended care from Massachusetts General Hospital until her passing.
Madison has been an oncology nurse for nearly two years, inspired by her mother's battle with cancer and MGH's support.

title: Boston police fatally shoot suspect allegedly wielding sword near Northeastern University campus
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston police fatally shot a man with a sword during a mental health crisis near Northeastern University.
The man stabbed a police officer and knocked down an EMS clinician before officers used tasers and guns.
Several officers and emergency responders were injured; the incident is under investigation by police and DA's office.

title: She’s running Boston to honor her godmother who died of brain cancer
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Abigail Dubois is running the 2026 Boston Marathon in memory of her godmother who died of brain cancer.
Dubois aims to support families by donating to the Cancer Care Equity program at Massachusetts General Hospital.
She qualified for the marathon in September 2025 and will run with the Massachusetts General Hospital team.

title: 1 dead, 2 hospitalized in fatal Brockton house fire, officials say
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: A Brockton house fire at 80 Glendale Ave. resulted in one death and two hospitalizations early Saturday morning.
Firefighters found intense flames engulfing the second floor where a deceased woman in her 40s was discovered.
The fire began in a second-floor bedroom; its cause appears unintentional but remains under investigation by authorities.

title: NH suspect who allegedly shot at family members killed in officer-involved shooting
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Matthew Masse, 38, was killed in a shootout with police in Raymond, NH after allegedly shooting at family.
The suspect wounded a Nottingham officer while police attempted to apprehend him; the officer's injuries are non-life-threatening.
Police set up a perimeter around Ham Road, leading to an evening shootout where Masse was killed.
